note: Simplified reference summaries. The FBC incorporates the IBC with Florida-specific amendments. Non-amended sections follow IBC 2021. For full code text, consult https://floridabuilding.org/ or https://codes.iccsafe.org/

FL_ADMIN.title: Florida Administrative Amendments
FL_ADMIN.summary: Florida-specific administrative provisions that override IBC Chapter 1. Includes permit requirements, inspection procedures, and local jurisdiction authority.

subsections.FL101.2: Scope - All buildings and structures in Florida except exempt structures per FS 553.73
subsections.FL104: Building official duties and qualifications per Florida Statute
subsections.FL105.1: Permits required - Building permits required for all construction except minor exempt work
subsections.FL105.2: Work exempt from permit - Ordinary repairs not exceeding $5,000, painting, carpeting, one-story detached accessory structures ≤200 sq ft
subsections.FL107: Plans and specifications - Signed and sealed by Florida-licensed architect or engineer for non-exempt buildings
subsections.FL110: Inspections - Foundation, framing, sheathing, insulation, lath/gypsum, final. Threshold buildings require special inspector

FL1609.title: Wind Loads (Florida Amendments)
FL1609.summary: Enhanced wind load requirements specific to Florida. More stringent than base IBC due to hurricane exposure. Includes High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) provisions for Miami-Dade and Broward counties.

subsections.FL1609.1: Applications - Design wind loads per ASCE 7 or Florida-specific provisions
subsections.FL1609.2: Protection of openings - Impact-resistant glazing or shutters required in wind-borne debris regions (within 1 mile of coast where V ≥ 130 mph, or entire HVHZ)
subsections.FL1609.3: Wind speed map - Florida-specific wind speed map per Figure 1609.3. Ultimate design wind speeds range from 115-190 mph depending on location and risk category
subsections.FL1609.4: Exposure category - Exposure B (urban), C (open terrain), D (coastal/flat open). Most coastal FL is Exposure C or D
subsections.FL1609.5: Roof deck attachment - Enhanced fastening schedules for Florida. 8d ring-shank nails at 6 in. OC edges typical for high-wind areas

FL1612.title: Flood Loads (Florida Amendments)
FL1612.summary: Enhanced flood load requirements for Florida's extensive coastal and flood-prone areas.

subsections.FL1612.1: General - Design for flood loads per ASCE 7, ASCE 24, and FBC floodplain management
subsections.FL1612.2: Definitions - Coastal construction control line (CCCL), coastal A zones, V zones
subsections.FL1612.4: Design and construction - Lowest floor elevated above BFE. A zones: BFE +1ft min. V zones: bottom of lowest horizontal structural member at or above BFE
subsections.FL1612.5: Flood-resistant materials - Below BFE, use flood damage-resistant materials per FEMA TB-2
subsections.FL1612.6: Manufactured homes - Must comply with HUD standards and local floodplain regulations

FL_HVHZ.title: High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ)
FL_HVHZ.summary: Special requirements for Miami-Dade and Broward counties. HVHZ has the most stringent wind requirements in the US, requiring product approval via Testing Application Standards (TAS).

subsections.HVHZ_scope: Applicability - Miami-Dade and Broward counties only. All products must have a Florida Product Approval (FPA) or Miami-Dade NOA
subsections.TAS_201: TAS 201 - Impact Test Procedures for Wind Resistive Components (large and small missile)
subsections.TAS_202: TAS 202 - Criteria for Testing Impact/Cycle Loading of Windows and Shutters (9 lb 2x4 at 34 fps large missile for residential, 50 fps for commercial)
subsections.TAS_203: TAS 203 - Criteria for Testing Products Subject to Cyclic Wind Pressure Loading
subsections.HVHZ_roofing: Roofing - Enhanced roof attachment, secondary water barrier required, roof-to-wall continuous load path connections
subsections.HVHZ_openings: Openings - All glazed openings must be impact-resistant or protected by approved shutters. Large missile test required
subsections.HVHZ_structural: Structural - Continuous load path from roof to foundation. All connections designed for uplift and lateral loads
subsections.HVHZ_product_approval: Product Approval - Products must be approved via Florida Product Approval system or local NOA (Notice of Acceptance). Search at https://floridabuilding.org/pr/pr_default.aspx

FL_MOISTURE.title: Florida Moisture Protection
FL_MOISTURE.summary: Florida-specific moisture barrier and waterproofing requirements for the hot-humid climate zone.

subsections.FL_moisture_1: Building envelope - Continuous water-resistive barrier required. Must address vapor drive from exterior to interior (opposite of cold climates)
subsections.FL_moisture_2: Vapor retarder - Vapor retarder on exterior of insulation NOT interior (hot-humid climate). Do not install vapor barrier on interior side in Florida
subsections.FL_moisture_3: Stucco systems - 2 layers of 60-minute Grade D building paper or equivalent WRB required behind stucco. Weep screed at base
subsections.FL_moisture_4: Window and door flashing - Pan flashing with end dams, sill flashing integrated with WRB, drainage capability required

FL_ENERGY.title: Florida Energy Code Amendments
FL_ENERGY.summary: Florida-specific energy efficiency requirements. Florida is Climate Zones 1 and 2 (hot-humid).

subsections.FL_energy_1: Climate zones - Zone 1 (Keys and south Florida), Zone 2 (central and north Florida)
subsections.FL_energy_2: Envelope - Roof R-38, wall R-13 (or R-13+5 continuous), floor R-13 min per Table C402.1.3
subsections.FL_energy_3: HVAC efficiency - Minimum SEER2 15.0 for split systems, 14.3 for package units (FL exceeds federal minimum)
subsections.FL_energy_4: Cool roof - Required for low-slope roofs in Climate Zones 1 and 2. Min initial SRI 78 (aged SRI 60)
subsections.FL_energy_5: Solar ready - New residential construction shall comply with solar-ready provisions per FL statute 553.894

FL_TERMITE.title: Florida Termite Protection
FL_TERMITE.summary: Termite protection requirements for Florida (very heavy termite hazard).

subsections.FL_termite_1: Subterranean termite protection - Soil treatment, physical barriers, or pressure-treated wood required for all structures
subsections.FL_termite_2: Methods - Soil chemical barrier (per NPMA), borate-treated wood, physical termite barriers, or approved monitoring systems
subsections.FL_termite_3: Drywood termites - South Florida requires additional drywood termite prevention measures

FL_ACCESSIBILITY.title: Florida Accessibility
FL_ACCESSIBILITY.summary: Florida accessibility requirements per Florida Statute 553.504 and the Florida Accessibility Code.

subsections.FL_access_1: Scope - Florida Accessibility Code applies to all public buildings and multi-family residential (4+ units)
subsections.FL_access_2: Vertical accessibility - Elevator required in multi-story buildings (with exemptions for <3 stories and <3,000 sq ft/floor)
subsections.FL_access_3: Fair housing - Multi-family housing with 4+ units and elevator OR ground floor units must comply with FHA accessibility requirements

_cross_reference.title: IBC 2021 Cross-Reference
_cross_reference.summary: For sections not specifically amended by Florida, refer to the IBC 2021 edition.

subsections.note_1: Chapters 2-6 (Use/Occupancy, Heights/Areas, Construction Types) - Generally follow IBC 2021 with minor FL amendments
subsections.note_3: Chapter 9 (Fire Protection Systems) - Follows IBC 2021 with FL fire marshal requirements
subsections.note_5: Chapters 19-23 (Concrete, Aluminum, Masonry, Steel, Wood) - Follow IBC 2021 with FL amendments for high-wind construction
